subtitles.original: 新しい未来を感じてる
subtitles.english: I can feel the new future.

subtitles.original: 怖くたって進むしかないでしょう
subtitles.english: Even if I'm scared, I have no choice but to move forward, right?

subtitles.original: 届けこの祈りよ 光となれ
subtitles.english: Reach out, my prayer! Become the light.

subtitles.original: 少しずつでもいい「私」という答えを
subtitles.english: Even if it's little by little, the answer called "myself"...

subtitles.original: もっと... もっと... もっと...
いつか空は灰になって落ちるって
subtitles.english: ...that someday the sky will turn to ash and fall.

subtitles.original: 妄想の世の中で日々を喰らっている
subtitles.english: I'm devouring my days in a world of delusions.

subtitles.original: 境界線を引いてしまうのも
今日、感覚のせいにして
街の灯の海で居場所を探している
subtitles.english: I'm searching for where I belong in a sea of city lights.

subtitles.original: 退屈に染まって動かない
subtitles.english: ...are stained with boredom and won't move.
Even though all I want to see...

subtitles.original: 景色が見たいだけなのにな
subtitles.english: ...is just a little bit of the scenery ahead.

subtitles.original: 雨が降ったら 雨が降ったら きっと
subtitles.english: When the rain falls, when the rain falls, surely...

subtitles.original: 枯れてしまった色ですら
目を瞑ったら 目を瞑ったら もっと
subtitles.english: If I close my eyes, if I close my eyes, even more...
...they will grow hazy in the distance.
The sound of the rain has become noisy.

subtitles.original: いつか溜息は夜に化けて歌を歌う
subtitles.english: ...that someday, our sighs will turn into night and sing.

subtitles.original: 妄想の世の中で日々を喰らっている
subtitles.english: We're devouring the days within a world of delusion.

subtitles.original: 離れる声に気付かない
夜が降ったら 夜が降ったら きっと
subtitles.english: When the night falls, when the night falls, surely...

subtitles.original: 満たされない日々の底で
subtitles.english: At the bottom of these unfulfilled days...

subtitles.original: 目を逸らしても 目を逸らしても ずっと
subtitles.english: Even if I look away, even if I look away, forever...
...in the gap between regret and sorrow...

subtitles.original: 取り残されてしまう前に
雨が降ったら 雨が降ったら きっと
subtitles.english: When the rain falls, when the rain falls, surely...

subtitles.original: 雨が降ったら 雨が降ったら きっと
subtitles.english: When the rain falls, when the rain falls, surely...

subtitles.original: 湿るアスファルトを背に映して俯いた
subtitles.english: I hung my head, with the damp asphalt reflected on my back.

subtitles.original: また明日に期待をして
subtitles.english: I place my hopes in tomorrow once again.

subtitles.original: 雨が降ったら 雨が降ったら きっと
subtitles.english: When the rain falls, when the rain falls, surely...

subtitles.original: 枯れてしまった色ですら
目を瞑ったら 目を瞑ったら もっと
subtitles.english: If I close my eyes, if I close my eyes, even more...
...they will grow hazy in the distance.
The sound of the rain has become noisy.

responses.thoughts:
**Subtitle Generation: A Deep Dive**

Okay, here's how I'm approaching this subtitle generation. The goal is clear: create synchronized, context-aware subtitles. I have the video, audio, visual text cues, and a `<scene_and_lyrics_reference_json>` for backup, and I'm laser-focused on the details. No ghost subs, strict timecode formatting, and contextually-accurate translations, particularly with pronouns and tense.

First, I'm dissecting the video into segments. It starts mid-song, so I pinpointed the exact starting lyrics in "Mirai Campanella". I precisely aligned the initial subtitle with the audio cue at 00:00.000, starting right on "くカンパネラ" ("ku kanpanera"). The analysis moves forward in an efficient line-by-line basis. This requires careful listening and cross-referencing against the auxiliary lyric data.

I had a minor hiccup with the lyrics. The reference JSON had discrepancies with the standard lyrics, so I'm meticulously checking the audio to confirm the correct transcription. I resolved the line using standard Balloon lyrics.

I flagged the instrumental sections, adhering to the "Zero Ghost Subtitles" rule. No subtitles during those parts. Then I identified a visual event: the song title "雨とペトラ" (Ame to Petra) at 01:07.500. It's a silent subtitle. I repeated the procedure for "群青讃歌" (Gunjo Sanka) at 04:46.000.

For "Ame to Petra", I'm now tracking vocal cues to accurately transcribe the lyrics. The timecodes are crucial. My timecode generation is designed for accuracy, matching start times to vocal activations, and using aggressive truncation to prevent any overlaps. I'm especially paying attention to repeating phrases and variations in emphasis within the lyrics. I also need to be careful with translation and continuity, adjusting translations as necessary.

Once all the lyrics are transcribed and timecoded, I'll go through a quality check. I'll make sure the timecode formatting is perfect (MM:SS.mmm). I will also confirm no overlap and the translations flow naturally in the English version. Then I need to confirm the "Zero Ghost Subtitles" rule is consistently followed. Instrumental sections should remain silent except when there's an explicit visual cue (like the title cards). Finally, I'll review the final JSON output to catch any formatting or logical errors before submitting the final subtitle file.
